issues.summary: [AU] E2E Segment Generation Validation — Pipeline Trigger & Size Consistency on PROD
issues.description:
### 1. Summary

Validate that the Optimal Reach segment generation pipeline triggers correctly on PROD for Australia and that segment sizes reported in Audience Builder / Pre-Campaign Insights / Post-Campaign Reporting are consistent with the ML job predictions.

This is a pipeline smoke test, not a full UAT. The goal is to confirm the end-to-end flow works in production before running comprehensive user acceptance testing.

### 2. Context

* AU infrastructure is in place:
* feature flags enabled <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-833</custom> 
* ACL migration deployed
* <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-832</custom> 
* Audience Builder ads section enabled <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-2">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-834</custom> 
* Data verification completed <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-3">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-860</custom> 
* AU uses HDMI Streaming Stick-based Ads Dataset + In-App ACR data for AVOD modeling

### 3. Scope

For 3-5 AU advertisers, trigger OR segment generation on PROD and compare the segment size output across:

| Check Point | Source | What to Compare |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ML Job output | ML pipeline | Predicted segment size from ML job |
| Audience Builder (AB) | DSP UI — Audience Builder | Segment size shown in AB after segment lands |
| Pre-Campaign Insights (AI) | DSP UI — OR Insights report | Segment size shown in pre-campaign report |
| Post-Campaign Reporting | DSP UI — Post-Campaign report | Segment size shown in post-campaign report (if applicable) |

**Test configurations** (follow Canada/ESBO pattern):

1. Linear Advertiser/Brand Only
2. Linear + Streaming Advertiser/Brand Only
3. Linear + Streaming mix

### 4. Steps

#### Step 1: Audience Builder, Segment Generation & Validation

1. Trigger OR segment generation via the ML pipeline (Airflow) for selected AU advertisers
2. Confirm the ML job completes successfully (no Airflow DAG failures)
3. Record the ML job predicted segment size
4. Navigate to Audience Builder confirm the generated segment appears for AU / Buyer 11
5. Record the segment size shown in AB
6. Compare AB segment size vs ML job output document discrepancy (≤5% tolerance = pass)

#### Step 2: Pre-Campaign Insights, Report Generation & Validation

1. Generate a Pre-Campaign Insights report for the same AU advertisers/brands
2. Confirm report generates successfully (no errors)
3. Record the segment size shown in the OR Insights report
4. Compare Pre-Campaign Insights segment size vs ML job output document discrepancy (≤5% tolerance = pass)

#### Step 3: Post-Campaign Reporting,  Report Pull & Output Check

1. Pull a Post-Campaign report for an AU advertiser with campaign delivery data (if available)
2. Confirm report generates successfully (no errors)
3. Verify incremental reach metrics and segment sizes are populated
4. Check outputs for data completeness and correctness

#### Step 4: Cross-Validation, AB vs Pre-Campaign Insights Size Comparison

1. For the same input data (same advertiser, same brand, same time window), compare segment sizes between Audience Builder and Pre-Campaign Insights
2. Document any discrepancies between AB and AI sizes
3. If difference >5%, investigate root cause (e.g., AD filtering logic, country filtering, exposure window differences)

Test records Confluence page: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-4">https://adgear.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/AGILE/pages/22925705410/AU+E2E+Segment+Generation+Validation+Pipeline+Trigger+Size+Consistency+on+PROD</custom> 

### 5. Acceptance Criteria

#### Step 1: Audience Builder

- [ ] ML pipeline triggers successfully for AU on PROD (no failures)
- [ ] Generated segments land in Audience Builder for AU / Buyer 11
- [ ] Segment size shown in AB matches ML job output within ≤5% tolerance
- [ ] If discrepancy >5%, root cause is documented

#### Step 2: Pre-Campaign Insights

- [ ] Pre-Campaign Insights report generates successfully for AU advertisers (no errors)
- [ ] Segment size shown in Pre-Campaign Insights matches ML job output within ≤5% tolerance
- [ ] If discrepancy >5%, root cause is documented

#### Step 3: Post-Campaign Reporting

- [ ] Post-Campaign report generates successfully for AU (no errors)
- [ ] Incremental reach metrics and segment sizes are populated and non-zero
- [ ] Report outputs are complete (no missing fields or broken calculations)

#### Step 4: Cross-Validation (AB vs Pre-Campaign Insights)

- [ ] For same input data, AB and Pre-Campaign Insights segment sizes compared
- [ ] Discrepancy between AB and AI is within ≤5% tolerance
- [ ] If discrepancy >5%, root cause is documented (known filtering differences are acceptable if explained)

### 6. Out of Scope

* Full UAT testing (separate ticket, later phase)
* Performance/latency benchmarking
* Multi-buyer validation (only Buyer 11 for now)
* Beta client campaign execution

---

### Key Observations from Canada

From the Canada test, typical results were:

* **Green (pass):** ≤2.5% difference between AI and AB
* **Yellow (pass with note):** 5-7% difference — usually explained by AD country filtering logic (AB filters by report country, AI filters by exposure country)
* **Expected zero cases:** Some advertisers had 0 segments in both AI and AB due to missing exposure data for the target country — valid but should be documented

issues.summary: [SRPOL-OR] Add dedicated cost tracking view for OR
issues.description:
As a developer, I want to reliably track Optimal Reach budget spend. Current methodology ([link](https://ads-ams25.slack.com/archives/C0AJW8F9V1N/p1776877262924209?thread_ts=1776872481.912619&cid=C0AJW8F9V1N)) lacks granulation and doesn’t provide enough insight. I want to create new view that would provide more information. This view should be based on information from DATAGOV_DB.DATAGOV_CATALOG.VIEW_QUERY_HISTORY_WEEKLY, udw_prod.udw_platform_intelligence_cs.pi_query_attribution_history and UDW_PROD.UDW_PLATFORM_INTELLIGENCE_CS.PI_QUERY_HISTORY views and be accessible both on EU and US.

**Acceptance criteria:**

* Create a new Optimal Reach cost tracking UDW view providing information such as number of segments ran, spend divided between PROD and DEV, total segment queued and execution time and any other information that will be deemed necessary
* Data is avaliable longer than week - no retencion
* Costs can be matched to processing jobs and payloads
* Actual number of dolars spent
* Data is loaded automatically
